title: Rust CLI Hello World Implementation

steps.description: Create domain layer Greeting entity with value type constraints
steps.done_condition: Greeting struct defined in domain/entities.rs with no external dependencies

steps.description: Create ports layer OutputPort trait for text output abstraction
steps.done_condition: OutputPort trait defined in ports/mod.rs accepting Greeting parameter

steps.description: Implement StdoutPrinter adapter for console output
steps.done_condition: StdoutPrinter struct implements OutputPort trait without external adapter imports

steps.description: Wire usecase logic to compose port and adapter
steps.done_condition: usecases/mod.rs successfully composes Greeting with OutputPort interface
Main application entry point registers adapters to ports

mergeOrder: domain layer merge first, then ports, then primary adapters, then usecases, finally composition root

riskRegister.mitigation: Accept larger binary size as documentation benefit
riskRegister.risk: Rust compilation overhead for simple output

riskRegister.mitigation: Validate hex framework cross-language support via ADR-018
Use scaffolding as documented baseline for future Rust modules

successCriteria: Hello message prints to console via StdoutPrinter adapter
Domain Greeting entity remains isolated from output implementation
OutputPort trait enables swapping adapters without changing usecase
All layers compile without cross-layer imports violating hex boundaries
